mood.label: Uneasy calm
mood.summary: A world at 53/100 — light-based computing breakthroughs and a tentative Hormuz opening nudge upward while climate alarms hit 39/100 and conflict casualties mount past 420, leaving no comfortable resting place.

lines: The air above South Asia holds its heat like a question,
thirty-nine in the climate column — alarming, not improving.
A threshold we keep naming but can't cross back from,
while the forecast turns red and the summer keeps moving.
In Osaka light learns to carry a little thought,
a photon threading logic where electrons used to go.
Four hundred names a ceasefire didn't hold —
and the record Nikkei climbs and doesn't want to know.
What the heat remembers the ledger won't show,
the degree-and-a-half we traded for a comfortable year.
Tech and hope on one side, fire on the other —
fifty-three out of a hundred, and we're still here.
